result:
**Queue** — point-to-point. One message, one consumer. Use when work must be processed exactly once by a single worker (job processing, task distribution, load balancing across workers).

**Topic** — publish/subscribe. One message, many consumers. Use when multiple independent systems need the same event (order placed → billing, inventory, analytics, email all react).

Rule of thumb: if you'd duplicate the message to fan it out, use a topic. If consumers compete for work, use a queue.

Modern brokers (Kafka, Pulsar, SNS+SQS) often blur the line — Kafka topics with consumer groups give you both: fan-out across groups, load-balanced within a group.

**Queue** — point-to-point. One message, one consumer. Use when work must be processed exactly once by a single worker (job processing, task distribution, load balancing across workers).

**Topic** — publish/subscribe. One message, many consumers. Use when multiple independent systems need the same event (order placed → billing, inventory, analytics, email all react).

Rule of thumb: if you'd duplicate the message to fan it out, use a topic. If consumers compete for work, use a queue.

Modern brokers (Kafka, Pulsar, SNS+SQS) often blur the line — Kafka topics with consumer groups give you both: fan-out across groups, load-balanced within a group.
